violations.text: The front lowboy was observed in poor repair (gasket not properly fitted/sealed). The equipment must be in good repair and proper adjustment. Equipment components such as doors, seals, hinges, fasteners, and kick plates shall be kept intact, tight, and adjusted in accordance with manufacturer's specifications. New Violation. Correct By: 10/27/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine has an accumulation of pink residue. The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine must be cleaned as often as necessary to prevent the contamination of ice. New Violation. Correct By: 10/27/2025

Several Food Items in the Stacked 2 Door Reach-In Cooler were observed held at 38 to 48 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food items observed at 45 degrees Fahrenheit and above were voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection (see temperatures and disposal). Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/27/2023

The door to the outside, located in the back storage area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 10/11/2023

The following equipment located in the kitchen area was observed in poor repair... - The stacked 2 door reach-in cooler was observed to be maintaining an ambient temperature of 40 to 48 degrees Fahrenheit from top to bottom. Use of the unit is to be discontinued until serviced/repaired. - The lower door to the stacked 2 door reach-in cooler was observed to be misaligned causing a gap and not sealing properly. - The Low boy freezer unit door was observed to be misaligned. New Violation. Correct By: 10/11/2023

The wall in the front ice cream/coffee area is in poor repair. Tiles were observed to be missing and loose on the wall. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/27/2023
